summary: Stage/ground-directory matching slice split from PR #2617. It made small source-shape/codegen improvements across src/melee/gr, producing decomp-dev gains of +3188 matched bytes, 3 new matches, and 15 unmatched-item improvements. The final slice touched GR stage files such as granime, grbigblue, grcastle, grcorneria, grgreens, grinishie1/2, grmutecity, ground, grrcruise, grvenom, and grzebes. Review caught a gricemt HSD_ASSERT change as a regression; gricemt is not present in the final changed_files list, suggesting that problematic slice content was removed or not part of the merged final diff.
what_changed: Split the GR/stage portion of a larger matching branch into an isolated src/melee/gr PR for easier review.
decomp-dev reported 3 new matches: grInishie2_801FD9EC, Ground_801C1E94, and grBigBlue_801E8D64.

decomp-dev reported 15 improvements in unmatched items, including grBigBlue_801E6C60, grMuteCity_801F1A34, grCastle_801CFBD4, grCorneria_801E1348, Ground_801C466C, grInishie1_801FB0AC, grAnime_801C7C1C, grRCruise_801FFADC, grZebes_801D9798, and several grVenom functions.
grAnime_801C7C1C moved HSD_MatAnim and HSD_ShapeAnimDObj locals earlier and replaced conditional expressions with explicit if/else assignments before calling grAnime_801C683C_noinline.
grBigBlue_801E6C60 corrected repeated access from gp->gv.bigblue.data[i].x8 to x4 and introduced left_x/right_x temporaries around grBigBlue_801E8B84 call shaping.
grCastle_801CFBD4 replaced direct repeated grCs_803B7F28/grCs_803B7F3C indexing with cb1_src/cb2_src local pointers while preserving stack padding.
grGreens_80214FA8 renamed grid loops from k/j to row/col and propagated row/col through grGreens_80215358 calls; assertion spacing was normalized.
grInishie1_801FB0AC removed an extra bool match flag and used the loop index reaching 0x13 as the condition for no active status-3 block.
grInishie2_801FD9EC assigned temp_vec to &gp->gv.inishie23.xD8 before HSD_JObjGetTranslation, completing a new match.

grMuteCity_801F1A34 removed a speed function pointer iterator and indexed ((grMc_SpeedFn*) &grMc_803E3B7C[30])[var_r27] directly; it also used explicit u32 null checks on pointer-like integer fields x24/x28 and changed a mutecity flag access from xD0_flags.b6 to xD0_flags.b1.

Ground_801C1E94/ground.c introduced archive and stage_id locals around grDatFiles_801C6324 and stage_info.internal_stage_id, changed integer 1 to 1.0F for a float, and in Ground_801C466C used assignment-in-condition plus a Vec3* sp10p local for light position/interest setters.
grRCruise_801FFADC used assignment-in-condition patterns when loading hsd_obj from ground objects before setting translations.

grVenom changes removed redundant scale temporaries in grVenom_80205AD4 and grVenom_80206874, used typed s32* base/data indexing instead of byte-pointer arithmetic in grVenom_80206874, and removed an unused base_b in grVenom_80205F30.
grZebes_801D9798 folded delay_max assignment into the comparison against delay_min to improve codegen.
smart_moves: Kept the PR limited to one subsystem/directory, which made a broad matching branch reviewable and allowed per-slice decomp-dev reporting.
Used small local variables to force desired register/stack behavior without changing semantics, such as archive, stage_id, sp10p, left_x/right_x, cb1_src/cb2_src, side, and lobj2.
Converted byte-offset arithmetic in grVenom_80206874 into typed s32* indexing, which is both clearer and a codegen aid when the underlying table behaves like words.
Removed unnecessary state variables where the loop index already carried the result, as in grInishie1_801FB0AC replacing bool match with i == 0x13.
Preserved or added PAD_STACK where needed instead of relying on incidental padding locals.
Used assignment inside conditionals in several places to match compiler structure while reducing extra statement shape.
Responded to review-identified regression risk around gricemt by not carrying gricemt into the final changed_files list.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, declaration order and local lifetime can matter as much as expression semantics; moving locals to function scope or nearer use can shift codegen favorably.
Explicit if/else assignments can match better than ternary expressions even when semantically equivalent.
Typed table pointers can replace raw u8* byte arithmetic when the disassembly pattern is actually word-indexed; this improves readability and may improve matching.
Do not delete or alter asserts casually for a match. A reviewer flagged removal of HSD_ASSERT(0x258, i<ICEMT_FIELD_MAX) in gricemt as a regression.
When pointer-like fields are stored in integer slots, explicit casts such as (u32) field != 0 may be needed for matching and to document the intended codegen shape.
Loop variable names that reflect domain axes, such as row and col, make grid logic easier to audit without disturbing matching if declarations are controlled.
Comparisons can be shaped by ordering constants on the left, folding assignments into conditions, or changing literal types such as 1 to 1.0F.
naming_conventions: Use row and col for 2D grid traversal instead of generic k/j when indexing row * 6 + col.
Use left_x and right_x for bounds passed to functions such as grBigBlue_801E8B84.
Use cb1_src and cb2_src for local aliases to static callback tables grCs_803B7F28 and grCs_803B7F3C.
Use side for gp->gv.corneria.x11A when it controls left/right branch logic.
Use lobj2 and j when a nested light-object traversal would otherwise collide with outer lobj/i variables.
Use base and data as typed table pointer names in grVenom where the table is treated as s32 entries.
Existing anonymous field names such as x4, x24, x28, xC4, xD0_flags remain heavily used; the PR did not broadly rename structs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move temporary declarations earlier or later to influence register allocation and stack layout.
Replace ternary expressions with explicit if/else assignments for noinline call arguments.
Use assignment-in-condition forms such as if ((jobj = gobj->hsd_obj) != NULL).
Introduce pointer aliases to stack locals, such as sp10p = &sp10, before repeated setter calls.
Use explicit float literals like 1.0F instead of integer literals where a float value is assigned.
Cast integer-backed pointer fields to u32 for null comparisons before casting back to HSD_GObj* for calls.
Use typed pointer indexing, e.g. s32* data with data[11], data[14], and data2[0xC9], instead of u8* plus byte offsets.
Fold assignment into comparison, as in assigning delay_max inside the grZebes delay_max > delay_min condition.
Remove redundant locals such as scl, base_b, fn_ptr, and bool match when direct expressions or loop indices match better.
Preserve explicit PAD_STACK calls when stack shape is part of the match.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged removal/change around HSD_ASSERT(0x258, i<ICEMT_FIELD_MAX) in grIceMt_801F686C as a regression.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for gricemt HSD_ASSERT ICEMT_FIELD_MAX to see where the regression was fixed or avoided.
Check whether gp->gv.bigblue.data[i].x4 has since received a semantic struct field name.
Look for later struct typing of grVe_803E5348 based on the s32* indexing patterns introduced here.
Review subsequent grmutecity work for naming of x24/x28 pointer-like fields and xD0_flags.b1.
